# Disclaimer

Copyright © 2010 – 2025 Open Charge Alliance. All rights reserved.

This document is made available under the Creative Commons Attribution-NoDerivatives 4.0 International Public License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/4.0/legalcode).

# Version History

- Version: 2.0.1 Edition 4; Date: 2025-12-03; Description: OCPP 2.0.1 Edition 4. All errata from OCPP 2.0.1 Part 5 until and including Errata 2025-11 have been merged into this version of the specification. In this edition all certification profiles are available.
- Version: 2.0.1 Edition 3; Date: 2024-05-06; Description: OCPP 2.0.1 Edition 3. All errata from OCPP 2.0.1 Part 5 until and including Errata 2024-04 have been merged into this version of the specification. In this edition all certification profiles are available.
- Version: 2.0.1; Date: 2023-06-30; Description: Release for Core & Advanced Security

# Chapter 1. Introduction & Reading Guide

This document describes the certification profiles for OCPP 2.0.1. These profiles are sets of use cases that can be certified via the Open Charge Alliance. This document contains the details on what is part of the OCPP 2.0.1 Certification. This document contains:

- The certification profiles and an overview of the functionality per profile.
- The list of optional features. This list contains specific functionality that is not mandatory for certification, but which can optionally be certified.
- The list of test cases for each of the certification profiles.
- The overview of the controller components that must be implemented per profile for certification testing.

For clarity: in the context of the OCPP Certification Program, the term test case refers to a sequence of messages for testing a use case from OCPP. The term feature refers to a functionality, that can be tested with one or more test cases (see Features for a more detailed explanation). Instead of making specific test cases mandatory or optional, the certification program for OCPP 2.0.1 works with features that are optional. Depending on whether the System Under Test (SUT) has implemented a feature, the test case(s) that belong to this feature, must be successfully passed or not.

- OCPP Specification: Part 2; Test cases are written: based on use cases & requirements; OCPP Specification: Part 6; Test cases are translated: in machine readable format (TTCN-3); TTCN-3 Test cases are: Test System executed using a Test Test Cases System
- OCPP Specification: OCPP uses cases & requir; Test cases are written: Starting point
Figure 1. Link between different OCPP Documents in OCPP Certification Program

# Chapter 2. Certification profiles

The OCPP protocol has been designed to support a wide variety of charging stations ranging from simple AC home chargers to advanced DC hyperchargers and megawatt chargers. It will be obvious that these charging stations and associated CSMSs will have very different capabilities. As a result it does not make sense to require every vendor to certify for the full OCPP functionality, when only subset is needed for the specific application.

The OCPP certification is built around certification profiles that describe a set of supported functions. A full OCPP certification comprises all certification profiles, but it is possible to get certified for a subset, since not all OCPP functionality may be needed for some vendors.

The OCPP "Core" profile must always be present. It contains the basic OCPP functionality. On top of that other profiles can be added to the certification. These profiles are independent of each other, the only exception being the "ISO 15118 support" profile, which requires a number of "Advanced security" and "Smart charging" test cases to be implemented.

The following table lists the certification profiles and an overview of the functionality per profile:

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

Core: Basic Authentication TLS - server-side certificate Update Charging Station Password for HTTP Basic Authentication Security Event Notification Booting a Charging Station Configuring a Charging Station Resetting a Charging Station / EVSE Authorization incl. GroupId Stop Transaction with a Master Pass Local start transaction - Cable plugin first & Authorization first Start / Stop transaction options Disconnect cable on EV-side Check Transaction status Remote start / stop transaction Remote unlock Connector Remote Trigger Change Availability - Charging Station / EVSE / Connector Clock-aligned Meter & Sampled Meter Values Install CA certificates Retrieve & delete certificates from Charging Station AdditionalRootCertificateCheck Retrieve Log Information Get / Clear Customer Information Secure Firmware Update Store / Clear Authorization Data in Authorization Cache Authorization through authorization cache Local Authorization List Management Authorization through local authorization list Send / Get Local Authorization List Advanced Device Management Get / Monitoring report Set Monitoring Base Set Variable Monitoring Set Monitoring Level Get Custom Report Clear / Remove Monitoring Event Notification Advanced User Interface Set / Get Display Message Clear a Display Message Show EV Driver Running / Final Total Cost During / After Charging Show EV Driver-specific Tariff Information Update Tariff Information During Transaction Configure Fallback Tariff Information & Total Cost Message Reservation Reserve a specific EVSE Reserve an unspecified EVSE Reserve a connector with a specific type Reservations using GroupIdToken Cancel reservation of an EVSE

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

Advanced Security: TLS - Client-side certificate Update Charging Station Certificate Upgrade Charging Station Security Profile
Smart Charging: Set charging profile Remote start transaction with charging profile Get Composite Schedule Get Charging Profile Clear Charging Profile
ISO 15118 support: ISO 15118 Certificate Management: Update Charging Station Certificate (Contract) Certificate Installation / Update EV Get Certificate Status Install V2G / MO CA certificates Retrieve V2G / MO certificates from Charging Station Delete a certificate from a Charging Station ISO 15118 EIM / PnC Authorization: Authorization using External Identification Means Authorization using Contract Certificates ISO 15118 Smart Charging: Set charging profile Remote start transaction with charging profile Get Composite Schedule Get Charging Profile Clear Charging Profile Renegotiating a Charging Schedule ISO 15118 signed meter values

# Chapter 3. Features

The concept of certification profiles is not enough to cover the variety in OCPP implementations. The OCPP specification contains
many optional features, often in the form of optional message fields or configuration variables, that a vendor can use to support
advanced functions. Whereas the certification profiles determine which OCPP functionality is implemented, the features describe
how much of a certain functionality in a profile has been implemented.

A Test System uses the features to determine which test cases have to be executed for a charging station or CSMS. For example,
the set of TxStartPoints that a charging station supports, has a big impact on the execution of certain test cases. The behavior of a
charging station that starts a transaction based on a successful authorization is different from a charging station that starts a
transaction as soon as a cable is connected. Similarly, a CSMS that only controls DC fast chargers will not need functionality to
unlock a cable at the charging station. For such a CSMS the vendor may decide to not implement the feature Support for unlocking
connector.

In most cases a feature corresponds the existence of a configuration variable or its value.

# 3.1. Optional feature list for charging station

The following table lists the optional features. These are features that are not mandatory to implement within a certification profile. Where applicable the associated configuration variable is mentioned in parenthesis.

- Id: C-01; Feature: Support for offline authorization of transactions; Charging Station: Conditional. Supporting this feature depends on whether at least one of the following feature combinations is supported; - Certification Profile: Local Authorization List Management AND at least one of the following local authorization options; C-30 or C-31 or C-32 - C-02: Support for allowing offline authorization for unknown ids AND at least one of the following local authorization options; C-30 or C-31 or C-32 or C-33 or C-34 - C-49: Authorization Cache AND at least one of the following local authorization options; C-30 or C-31 or C-32 - C-35: Local Authorization - NoAuthorization - start (Because there is no authorization, no local authorization mechanism is needed.)
- Id: C-02; Feature: Support for allowing Offline Authorization for Unknown Ids; Charging Station: Optional (OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled)
- Id: C-03; Feature: Support for maximizing energy for invalid ids; Charging Station: Optional (MaxEnergyOnInvalidId)

# C-40.5 AlignedDataTxEndedMeasurands

(Please fill in the following fields)

- { list of supported } at least one
- { list of supported } at least one
- { list of supported } at least one
- { list of supported } at least one
- { list of supported } at least one

# C-41 Supported Cipher Suites

- Id: C-41; Feature: Supported Cipher Suites; Charging Station: { list of cipher suites } → at least one of

# C-42 to C-75 Features

- C-42 Signed Metervalues (SampledDataSignReadings) - Optional
- C-43 Install Firmware with ongoing transaction(s) - Optional
- C-47 Support for falling back to default OCPP reconnection mechanism when NetworkConnection profile connection has failed - Optional
- C-48 Authorization of remote start (AuthorizeRemoteStart) - (At least one of the suboptions below is required)
- - C-48.1 Option for authorization in case of a remote start - Optional
- C-48.2 Option for no authorization in case of a remote start - Optional

C-58 Option for disabling remote authorization (DisableRemoteAuthorization) - Optional
- C-49 Authorization Cache (AuthCacheEnabled) - Optional
- C-59 Option for disabling remote authorization for cached invalid idTokens (AuthCacheDisablePostAuthorize) - Optional
- C-51 Configurable TxStartPoint - Optional
- C-52 Configurable TxStopPoint - Optional
- C-53 Support for lifetime cached token (AuthCacheLifeTime) - Optional
- C-54 Supported policies for replacing cached entries (AuthCachePolicy) - { list of supported } at least one
- C-56 Support for providing the SummaryInventory - Optional
- C-57 Support for cancelling ongoing log file upload - Optional
- C-60 Support for cancelling ongoing firmware update - Optional
- C-61 Security Profile 1 - Unsecured Transport with Basic Authentication - Optional
- Authorization options for local stop
- C-70 Authorization - using RFID ISO14443 - Optional
- C-71 Authorization - using RFID ISO15693 - Optional
- C-72 Authorization - using KeyCode - Optional
- C-75 Authorization - NoAuthorization - Optional

# Reservation

- R-0: R-1; Support for Reservation: Support for reservations of connectorType; Optional: Conditional.
- R-0: Supporting this feature depends on whether at least one connectorType is supported that is part of the ConnectorEnumType list from part 2 specification.
- R-0: R-2; Support for Reservation: Support for reservation of unspecified EVSE (ReservationNonEvseSpecific); Optional: Optional
- R-0: R-3; Support for Reservation: Support for disabling Reservations (ReservationEnabled); Optional: Optional

# Advanced Device Management

- DM-0: DM-3; Support for Advanced Device Management: Queue notifyEventRequest messages for specific severities; Optional: Optional
- DM-0: (OfflineMonitoringEventQueuingSeverity)

# Local Authorization List Management

- LA-0: LA-1; Support for Local Authorization List Management: Authorization list support (LocalAuthListEnabled); Optional: Optional
- LA-0: LA-3; Support for Local Authorization List Management: Option for disabling remote authorization for invalid idTokens stored at the Local Authorization List (LocalAuthListDisablePostAuthorize); Optional: Optional

# Smart Charging

- SC-2: SC-2.1; Supported charging rate units (ChargingScheduleChargingRateUnit): A; (Select all supported suboptions): Optional
- SC-2: SC-2.2; Supported charging rate units (ChargingScheduleChargingRateUnit): W; (Select all supported suboptions): Optional

# ISO 15118 support

- ISO-4: ISO-5; Support for retrieving / deleting CustomerInformation - CustomerCertificate: Charging Station can provide a contract certificate that it cannot validate to the CSMS; Optional: Optional

# Table 3. Optional features for CSMS

- Id: C-11; Feature: Support for unlocking connector for charging station with detachable cable (UnlockConnector message).; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-13; Feature: Support for Reset per EVSE; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-14; Feature: Support for retrieving / deleting CustomerInformation - CustomerIdentifier; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-15; Feature: Support for scheduled firmware updates; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-16; Feature: Support for checking the TransactionStatus; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-17; Feature: Support for retrieving the ConfigurationInventory; CSMS: Optional

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Id: C-29; Feature: TriggerMessage; CSMS: (Select all supported suboptions)
- Id: C-29.1; Feature: Trigger message - MeterValues; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-29.2; Feature: Trigger message - TransactionEvent; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-29.3; Feature: Trigger message - LogStatusNotification; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-29.4; Feature: Trigger message - FirmwareStatusNotification; CSMS: Optional
- Id: C-29.5; Feature: Trigger message - StatusNotification; CSMS: Optional

# Authorization options for local start

- C-30: C-31; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using RFID ISO15693; Required: Required
- C-30: C-32; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using KeyCode; Required: Optional
- C-30: C-33; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using locally generated id; Required: Optional
- C-30: C-34; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - MacAddress; Required: Optional
- C-30: C-35; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - NoAuthorization; Required: Required

# Authorization options for remote start (mandatory to support at least one)

- C-36: C-37; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using RFID ISO15693; Required: Required
- C-36: C-38; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using centrally, in the CSMS (or other server) generated id; Required: Optional
- C-36: C-39; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - NoAuthorization; Required: Optional

content:
# 4.1. Introduction

This table lists the test cases that are part of the OCPP Certification program. For each of the test cases, the columns "Conf. Test for Charging Station" and "Conf. Test for CSMS" indicate whether the test case is mandatory or not within a Certification Profile. The abbreviations have the following meaning:

- M = Mandatory. This means that IF you implement the certification profile this test case belongs to, you MUST successfully pass this test case.
- C = Conditional. This means that IF you meet a condition, you MUST pass this test case. Most conditions refer to the optional features that are listed in the Features.

# 4.2. Test Cases Core

- TC Id: TC_A_01; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Valid username/password combination; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Feature: Basic Authentication
- TC Id: TC_A_02; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Username does not equal ChargingStationId; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M
- TC Id: TC_A_03; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Invalid password; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M
- TC Id: TC_A_09; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Accepted; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Feature: Update Charging Station Password for HTTP Basic Authentication
- TC Id: TC_A_10; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Rejected; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M; Conf. test for CSMS: M
- TC Id: TC_A_04; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Valid certificate; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Feature: TLS - server-side certificate
- TC Id: TC_A_05; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Invalid certificate; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M
- TC Id: TC_A_06; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: TLS version too low; Conf. Test for Charging Station: M; Conf. test for CSMS: M
- TC Id: TC_A_19; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Accepted; Conf. Test for Charging Station: C; Conf. test for CSMS: C; Condition / remark: C-61 OR Advanced Security; Feature: Upgrade Charging Station Security Profile

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Test Case: TC_A_20; Description: No valid CSMSRootCertificate installed; Severity: C; Notes: If the last CSMSRootCertificate can be removed and Security Profile 1 is supported.; References: AQ-1 and C-61
- Test Case: TC_A_22; Description: Downgrade security profile - Rejected; Severity: M; Notes: Cold Boot Charging Station
- Test Case: TC_B_01; Description: Accepted; Severity: M; Notes: M
- Test Case: TC_B_02; Description: Pending; Severity: M; Notes: C; References: CSMS: If Pending mechanism is implemented
- Test Case: TC_B_03; Description: Rejected; Severity: M
- Test Case: TC_B_30; Description: Pending/Rejected - SecurityError; Severity: M; Notes: C; References: For CSMS: if CSMS can be configured to first respond to a BootNotificationRequest with status Pending or Rejected
- Test Case: TC_B_31; Description: Pending/Rejected - TriggerMessage; Severity: C; Notes: For CSMS: if CSMS can be configured to first respond to a BootNotificationRequest with status Pending or Rejected
- Test Case: TC_B_51; Description: > Offline Threshold; Severity: M
- Test Case: TC_B_52; Description: < Offline Threshold; Severity: M
- Test Case: TC_B_06; Description: single value; Severity: M; Notes: M
- Test Case: TC_B_07; Description: multiple values; Severity: M; Notes: C; References: If the CSMS supports multiple elements in a GetVariablesRequest

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Test Case: TC_B_47; Description: Fallback after NetworkProfileConnectionAttempts per NetworkConfigurationPriority failed - New CSMS; Severity: C; Requirements: For CS: at least two configuration slots for networkConnectionProfiles must be supported; Notes: AS-2 and C-47 Additional Root Certificate check mechanism implemented & Reconnect after NetworkProfileConnectionAttempts
- Test Case: TC_B_49; Description: Fallback after NetworkProfileConnectionAttempts per NetworkConfigurationPriority failed - Same CSMS; Severity: C; Requirements: For CS: at least two configuration slots for networkConnectionProfiles must be supported; Notes: C-47 Reconnect after NetworkProfileConnectionAttempts
- Test Case: TC_B_50; Description: Success - New CSMS Root - New CSMS Network Reconnection; Severity: M; Requirements: For CS: at least two configuration slots for networkConnectionProfiles must be supported
- Test Case: TC_B_57; Description: After connection loss; Severity: M
- Test Case: TC_B_58; Description: WebSocket Subprotocol negotiation; Severity: M; Notes: Local start transaction
- Test Case: TC_C_02; Description: Authorization Invalid/Unknown; Severity: C; Requirements: M; Notes: Charging Station: (C-30 or C-31 or C-32) Local Authorization - using RFID
- Test Case: TC_C_06; Description: Authorization Blocked; Severity: C; Requirements: M; Notes: For CS: NOT AQ-2 and (C-30 or C-31 or C-32) Local Authorization - using RFID

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Test Case: TC_E_38; Description: EV not ready; Condition: C M; Options: For CS: not supporting start transaction; Authorization Method: NOT C-09.5 and NOT Start transaction options - EnergyTransfer
- Test Case: TC_C_56; Description: Authorization Unknown; Condition: C; Options: Charging Station: - The Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32; Authorization Method: Local Authorization - using RFID and NOT AQ-2 ISO14443 / RFID ISO15693 / KeyCode
- Test Case: TC_C_05; Description: Authorization invalid - Cable lock; Condition: C; Options: For CS: - The Charging Station has a cable lock, which prevents the EV driver to connect the EV and EVSE before authorization. - The Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options C-30, C-31, C-32 - The Charging Station does NOT have the following configuration: TxStartPoint ReadOnly AND value Authorized is NOT set.; Authorization Method: Local Authorization - using RFID ISO14443 / RFID ISO15693 / KeyCode
- Test Case: TC_C_04; Description: Different idToken; Condition: C; Options: Charging Station: - The Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-70, C-71, C-72; Authorization Method: Local Authorization - using RFID ISO14443 / RFID ISO15693 / KeyCode
- Test Case: TC_E_06; Description: Accepted; Condition: C; Options: The Charging Station supports E07 Transaction locally stopped by IdToken with at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-70, C-71, C-72, C-75; Authorization Method: Local Authorization - using RFID ISO14443 / RFID ISO15693 / KeyCode / NoAuthorization

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Feature ID: TC_C_26; Description: Unknown Id; Status: C; Notes: If the feature Unknown Offline Authorization is supported AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32, C-33, C-34
- Feature ID: TC_C_47; Description: With UI - All transactions; Status: C; Notes: CS: If the feature Master Pass with UI is supported AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-70, C-71, C-72
- Feature ID: TC_C_48; Description: With UI - Specific transactions; Status: C; Notes: CS: If the feature Master Pass with UI is supported AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-70, C-71, C-72
- Feature ID: TC_C_49; Description: Without UI; Status: C; Notes: CS: If the feature Master Pass with UI is supported AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-70, C-71, C-72
- Feature ID: Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache
- Feature ID: TC_C_32; Description: Persistent over reboot; Status: C; Notes: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Feature ID: TC_C_33; Description: Update on AuthorizeResponse; Status: C; Notes: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Feature ID: TC_C_34; Description: Update on TransactionResponse; Status: C; Notes: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Test Case: TC_C_08; Description: Accepted; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Test Case: TC_C_09; Description: Invalid & Not Accepted; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Test Case: TC_C_12; Description: Invalid & Accepted; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Test Case: TC_C_10; Description: Blocked; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Test Case: TC_C_11; Description: Expired; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37
- Test Case: TC_C_13; Description: Accepted but cable not connected yet.; Result: C; Conditions: If the Charging Station has an authorization cache AND the Charging Station supports at least one of the following local start authorization options: C-30, C-31, C-32 OR supports at least one of the following remote start authorization options: C-36, C-37

- TxStopPoint can either be ReadOnly with a subset of the values or have a valueList of supported values, that contains a subset. - Test Case: TC_L_11; Description: Unable to cancel; Mandatory: C; Conditional: M; Notes: The Charging Station does NOT support cancelling an ongoing firmware update
- Test Case: TC_L_18; Description: Missing firmware signing certificate and signature; Mandatory: M
- Test Case: TC_L_12; Description: Unable to download/install firmware with ongoing transaction - AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is true; Mandatory: C; Notes: AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is implemented. The Charging Station is unable to download AND install firmware while there is an ongoing transaction.
- Test Case: TC_L_13; Description: Unable to download/install firmware with ongoing transaction - AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is false; Mandatory: C; Conditional: M; Notes: AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is implemented. The Charging Station is unable to download AND install firmware while there is an ongoing transaction.
- Test Case: TC_L_14; Description: Unable to install and activate firmware with ongoing transaction - AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is true; Mandatory: C; Notes: AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is implemented. The Charging Station is unable to install firmware while there is an ongoing transaction.
- Test Case: TC_L_15; Description: Unable to install firmware with ongoing transaction - AllowNewSessionsPendingFirmwareUpdate is false; Mandatory: C; Notes: The Charging Station is unable to install firmware while there is an ongoing transaction.
- Test Case: TC_L_16; Description: Able to update firmware with ongoing transaction; Mandatory: C; Notes: If the Charging Station supports Install Firmware with ongoing transaction(s)

- Related features: With stackLevel/purpose combination for one profile; Test Case ID: TC_K_06; Requirement: M; Remark: M

# 4.5. Test Cases ISO 15118 Support

- TC Id: TC_A_12; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Success - V2G Certificate; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_C_50; OCPP Compliance Testing Tool scenario: Authorization using Contract Certificates 15118; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Online - Local contract certificate validation - Accepted; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_C_51; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Online - Local contract certificate validation - Rejected; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_C_52; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Online - Central contract certificate validation - Accepted; Conf. test for CSMS: C; Condition / remark: M; Feature no.: Applicable if the Charging Station supports ISO-5 central contract validation.
- TC Id: TC_C_53; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Online - Central contract validation fails; Conf. test for CSMS: C; Feature no.: Applicable if the Charging Station supports ISO-5 central contract validation.
- TC Id: TC_C_54; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Offline - ContractValidationOffline is true; Conf. test for CSMS: M
- TC Id: TC_C_55; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Offline - ContractValidationOffline is false; Conf. test for CSMS: M
- TC Id: TC_E_46; Conf. Test for Charging Station: End of charging process 15118; Conf. test for CSMS: M
- TC Id: TC_K_01; Conf. Test for Charging Station: Set Charging Profile; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_K_10; Conf. Test for Charging Station: TxDefaultProfile - All EVSE; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_K_60; Conf. Test for Charging Station: TxProfile with ongoing transaction on the specified EVSE; Conf. test for CSMS: M; Condition / remark: M
- TC Id: TC_K_02; Conf. Test for Charging Station: TxProfile without ongoing transaction on the specified EVSE; Conf. test for CSMS: M

# Certificate Installation EV

- TC_M_26: TC_M_27; Success: Failed

# Certificate Update EV

- TC_M_28: TC_M_29; Success: Failed

# Get Charging Station Certificate status

- TC_M_24: TC_M_25; Success: Rejected

# Clear Customer Information

If Support for retrieving / deleting CustomerInformation - CustomerCertificate is supported.

# Chapter 5. OCPP 2.0.1 Mandatory Controller components per profile

Controller components contain variables that describe the supported features of a Charging Station and influence its behavior. In OCPP 2.0.1 we have configuration variables that are required or optional, but these are contained by controller components. Functionalities cannot be tested without the accompanying controller component, so for certification the following controller components are mandatory:

Core: OCPPCommCtrlr TxCtrlr DeviceDataCtrlr ClockCtrlr SecurityCtrlr SampledDataCtrlr AlignedDataCtrlr AuthCtrlr
Core: DM-0: MonitoringCtrlr
Core: LA-0: LocalAuthListCtrlr
Core: UI-0: TariffCostCtrlr DisplayMessageCtrlr
Core: R-0: ReservationCtrlr
Advanced Security: SecurityCtrlr (already part of Core)
Smart Charging: SmartChargingCtrlr
ISO 15118 Support: ISO15118Ctrlr SmartChargingCtrlr

metadata.heading: Chapter 5. OCPP 2.0.1 Mandatory Controller components per profile
metadata.heading_path: Chapter 5. OCPP 2.0.1 Mandatory Controller components per profile

content:
# 6.1. Questions for Charging Stations

AQ-1: Can the last CSMSRootCertificate be removed?
AQ-2: Does the Charging Station have a cable lock, which prevents the EV driver to connect the EV and EVSE before authorization?
AQ-3: Can the last ChargingStationCertificate be removed (via other means than OCPP)?
AQ-4: Is there at least one unsupported NumberOfPhases?
AQ-5: Does the Charging Station have at least one hardWired monitor? If yes, which hardWired monitor should be used for the certification test
AQ-6: Does the Charging Station have a pre-configured monitor? If yes, which pre-configured monitor should be used for the certification test
AQ-7: Is your Charging Station able to download firmware while there is an ongoing transaction?
AQ-8: Does your Charging Station enforce a selection of EVSE (by design) prior to authorization?
AQ-9: Does your Charging Station support charging an EV using IEC 61851-1 (Mode 3)?
AQ-10: Does your Charging Station support setting a Delta monitor on the WriteOnly component.variable SecurityCtrlr.BasicAuthPassword?
AQ-11: Does your Charging Station support a combined charging station Certificate (for both OCPP and ISO 15118)
AQ-18: Does your Charging Station have at least one connector with an (automatic) mechanized locking mechanism on Charging Station side? (this is always true for connectorTypes; sType2 and sType3)

# 6.2. Questions for CSMSs

AQ-12: Is a FullInventory requested during onboarding / booting test cases?
AQ-13: Does your CSMS support Absolute values for the following Charging Profiles:
AQ-13.1: TxDefaultProfile
AQ-13.2: ChargingStationMaxProfile
AQ-14: Does your CSMS support Recurring values for the following Charging Profiles:
AQ-14.1: TxDefaultProfile
AQ-14.2: ChargingStationMaxProfile
AQ-16: Does the CSMS reject unknown Charging Stations during websocket connection setup?
AQ-17: Can your CSMS be configured to first respond to a BootNotificationRequest with status Pending or Rejected?

 Profiles

# Chapter 7. Appendix B: Hardware feature set

The table below gives an overview of the hardware feature set Ids that are used for determining whether test cases are needed / applicable for certification.

HFS-1: Charging Station has a detachable cable
HFS-2: Charging Station has a fixed cable
HFS-3: Charging Station has AC support
HFS-4: Charging Station has DC support
HFS-5: Charging Station has 1 phase support
HFS-6: Charging Station has 2 phase support
HFS-7: Charging Station has 3 phase support
HFS-8: No. EVSEs of Charging Station
HFS-9: Communication technology
HFS-10: RFID readers
HFS-11: DC power level (kW)
HFS-12: Number of displays

metadata.heading: Chapter 7. Appendix B: Hardware feature set
metadata.heading_path: Chapter 7. Appendix B: Hardware feature set

content:
# Chapter 8. Appendix C: Features vs. OCPP use cases

The table below gives an overview of the use cases / configuration variables that the features are applicable for / referring to in the OCPP 2.0.1 Specification Part 2.

- Id: Core; Feature: C-01 Support for offline authorization of transactions; Related use cases: C15, C10, C11, C12

- C-23: C-28; Supported time sources (TimeSource): Toggle sending clock aligned meter values when a transaction is ongoing / Idle; Configuration Variable for J01 (AlignedDataSendDuringIdle)

- C-30: C-35; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - NoAuthorization; C01: C02

- C-36: C-38; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - using centrally, in the CSMS (or other server) generated id; C01: C05
- C-36: C-39; Authorization - using RFID ISO14443: Authorization - NoAuthorization; C01: C02

# Part 5 - Certification Profiles

- Id: R-1; Feature: Support for reservations of connectorType; Related use cases: H01.S3
- Id: R-2; Feature: Support for reservations of unspecified EVSE (ReservationNonEvseSpecific); Related use cases: H01.S1
- Id: R-3; Feature: Support for disabling Reservations (ReservationEnabled); Related use cases: Configuration Variable for H01
- Id: **Advanced Device Management**
- Id: DM-0; Feature: Support for Advanced Device Management; Related use cases: Optional
- Id: DM-3; Feature: Queue notifyEventRequest messages for specific severities; Related use cases: Configuration Variable for N07 (OfflineMonitoringEventQueuingSeverity)
- Id: **Local Authorization List Management**
- Id: LA-0; Feature: Support for Local Authorization List Management; Related use cases: D01-D02
- Id: LA-1; Feature: Authorization list support (LocalAuthListEnabled); Related use cases: Configuration Variable for C13
- Id: LA-2; Feature: Support for GetLocalListVersion; Related use cases: D02
- Id: LA-3; Feature: Option for disabling remote authorization for invalid idTokens stored at the Local Authorization List; Related use cases: Configuration Variable for C14 (LocalAuthListDisablePostAuthorize)
- Id: **Advanced User Interface**
- Id: UI-0; Feature: Support for Advanced User Interface; Related use cases: N02-N15
- Id: UI-1; Feature: Supported message priorities (DisplayMessageSupportedPriorities); Related use cases: O01
- Id: UI-2; Feature: Supported message formats (DisplayMessageSupportedFormats); Related use cases: O01
